summary: A Go CLI monolith currently undergoing aggressive, automated test coverage boosting across its runner, schema, and command packages.
concerns: over-optimization for test coverage metrics rather than meaningful tests
proliferation of artificially named test files (coverage_boost, coverage_boost5)
scattered agent-generated test directories (GOrchestra/glm-agents) polluting the repository structure
potential brittle tests focused on hitting lines rather than validating behavior
suggestions: consolidate or clean up the GOrchestra/glm-agents generated test directories
refactor coverage_boost test files into logically named, behavior-focused test suites
shift focus from coverage percentage to mutation testing or integration test quality
resolve the untracked and modified files in the current git status
